description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a slightly bulging cylindrical shape, constructed from vertical wooden staves bound together by four evenly spaced metal hoops. The wood has a rich, dark brown finish with visible grain patterns and subtle weathering, suggesting age and outdoor exposure. The metal bands are slightly rusted, adding to the rustic and utilitarian appearance. The top surface is flat and appears to be made of the same wood as the sides, tightly sealed and flush with the rim. There are no visible handles, spouts, or openings, and the barrel is solidly built, likely intended for storage or transport of liquids or dry goods. The overall craftsmanship and materials indicate it would be commonly found in urban alleys, storage yards, or near garages.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with metal hoops, dark brown wood, and a flat, sealed top. The surface shows rustic wear and no visible handles or markings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full curvature of the barrel, with all four metal hoops and the wood grain clearly visible.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view highlighting the rounded profile and the subtle rust on the metal bands.
description_view_2: Another angled view, emphasizing the even spacing of the metal hoops and the seamless wooden staves.
description_view_3: Side view with a clear look at the continuous wood texture and the flat, closed top surface.
